content.problemStatement:
Chef has an array AAA of length NNN.

In one operation, Chef can:


Choose any two **distinct** indices i,ji, ji,j (1‚â§i,j‚â§N)(1\le i, j\le N)(1‚â§i,j‚â§N) and change both AiA_iAi‚Äã and AjA_jAj‚Äã to (Ai+Aj)(A_i + A_j)(Ai‚Äã+Aj‚Äã). Note that both the elements AiA_iAi‚Äã and AjA_jAj‚Äã are getting replaced by the same value.
Find the **minimum** number of operations required by Chef to make all the elements divisible by 333.

It is guaranteed that we can make all the elements divisible by 333 using some **finite** number of operations.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - denoting the length of array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶ANA_1, A_2, \dots A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶AN‚Äã.




Output Format
For each test case, output the **minimum** number of operations required by Chef to make all the elements divisible by 333.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 2‚ãÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚ãÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: We can make all the elements divisible by 333 using 222 operations.

Operation 111: Choose i=1i = 1i=1 and j=2j = 2j=2. Thus, we replace both A1A_1A1‚Äã and A2A_2A2‚Äã by A1+A2=4+8=12A_1+A_2 = 4+8 = 12A1‚Äã+A2‚Äã=4+8=12. The array becomes [12,12,20,16][12, 12, 20, 16][12,12,20,16].
Operation 222: Choose i=3i = 3i=3 and j=4j = 4j=4. Thus, we replace both A3A_3A3‚Äã and A4A_4A4‚Äã by A3+A4=20+16=36A_3+A_4 = 20+16 = 36A3‚Äã+A4‚Äã=20+16=36. The array becomes [12,12,36,36][12, 12, 36, 36][12,12,36,36].

All elements of the array are divisible by 333. It can be shown that this is the minimum number of operations required to do so.
Test case 222: We can make all the elements divisible by 333 using 666 operations.

Operation 111: Choose i=1i = 1i=1 and j=2j = 2j=2. Thus, we replace both A1A_1A1‚Äã and A2A_2A2‚Äã by A1+A2=4+3=7A_1+A_2 = 4+3 = 7A1‚Äã+A2‚Äã=4+3=7. The array becomes [7,7,6,9][7, 7, 6, 9][7,7,6,9].
Operation 222: Choose i=1i = 1i=1 and j=3j = 3j=3. Thus, we replace both A1A_1A1‚Äã and A3A_3A3‚Äã by A1+A3=7+6=13A_1+A_3 = 7+6 = 13A1‚Äã+A3‚Äã=7+6=13. The array becomes [13,7,13,9][13, 7, 13, 9][13,7,13,9].
Operation 333: Choose i=1i = 1i=1 and j=2j = 2j=2. Thus, we replace both A1A_1A1‚Äã and A2A_2A2‚Äã by A1+A2=13+7=20A_1+A_2 = 13+7 = 20A1‚Äã+A2‚Äã=13+7=20. The array becomes [20,20,13,9][20, 20, 13, 9][20,20,13,9].
Operation 444: Choose i=3i = 3i=3 and j=4j = 4j=4. Thus, we replace both A3A_3A3‚Äã and A4A_4A4‚Äã by A3+A4=13+9=22A_3+A_4 = 13+9 = 22A3‚Äã+A4‚Äã=13+9=22. The array becomes [20,20,22,22][20, 20, 22, 22][20,20,22,22].
Operation 555: Choose i=1i = 1i=1 and j=3j = 3j=3. Thus, we replace both A1A_1A1‚Äã and A3A_3A3‚Äã by A1+A3=20+22=42A_1+A_3 = 20+22= 42A1‚Äã+A3‚Äã=20+22=42. The array becomes [42,20,42,22][42, 20, 42, 22][42,20,42,22].
Operation 666: Choose i=2i = 2i=2 and j=4j = 4j=4. Thus, we replace both A2A_2A2‚Äã and A4A_4A4‚Äã by A2+A4=20+22=42A_2+A_4 = 20+22= 42A2‚Äã+A4‚Äã=20+22=42. The array becomes [42,42,42,42][42, 42, 42, 42][42,42,42,42].

All elements of the array are divisible by 333. It can be shown that this is the minimum number of operations required to do so.
